"Under the Dying Sun" (promo 1998) - This album included early takes of the songs later on to be featured on the demo "The Silent Cry". These early takes were done under pretty rough conditions and to be honest they sound rather horrible in terms of quality.

"The Silent Cry" (demo 1998) - This was the first "real" recording and it was done all by me. Even though it sounds way better than the early takes it has a pretty rough sound and the most interessting thing about the album is the idea behind the songs and not how they were performed.

"Promo '99" (promo 1999) - In order to present how the arrival of Micce had affected the sound of the band we decided to re-record the song "The Silent Cry" as well as covering Katatonia's "Rainroom". This album was primarily meant for media and record-companies which explains why we didn't release it properly.

"Promo 2000" (promo 2000) - Since we didn't have enough material for a new demo we wanted to give something to the audience in the meantime and thus we recorded yet another old . This was just like the predeccesing album done primarily for media and record-companies.

"where dreams turn to dust" (demo 2000/mCD 2001) - This was the first album since '98 to feature new material. It was written before Martin Claesson left the band which means that he was involved in its creation. We were pretty consumed by the popularity achieved by the promos and we thought that it wouldn't be hard to find a record-company to release this material as a proper album. After a while we realized that things didn't work that easy and it took about three months before the label Molded Skull contacted us and wanted to release us as their first album. Things didn't work out very well for Molded Skull though and after awhile we had to look for another label. Actually we didn't need to look very much since the english label Rage of Achilles has contacted us while we were signed to Molded Skull and we just contacted them again and asked if they still wanted to release our stuff and they were more than ok with that.

"conquest for the empire" (compilation 2002) - The song "wish" from the mCD was picked to end this compilation which features songs from most Rage of Achilles signed bands. Worth mentioning is that one of these bands are Shubend, another band of mine in which I am vocalist.
Who are the other members of Foreset of Shadows?
Right now the only member besides me is Micce Andersson who has been around since 1998.
How many times you are able to play in live in one year?
As it is right now we don't play live at all due to various reasons.
Can you tell me about the new upcoming album,when will be released?

The new, still untitled, album will be released late 2002/early 2003. The featured material will sound pretty much like the mCD but with much more structure and thought behind it. I guess fans of the mCD won't be disappointed and I hope that the improved sound will open up for more new fans.
Any future plans,maybe tour?

If things work well with the new album and if the right people are willing to help as session musicans then we might consider touring. It's nothing that we are planning for though but it would be very cool :) Apart from that we don't have any exact plans for the future. Both me and Micce are pretty busy individuals so we might need time for other things as well. There will be more albums from Forest of Shadows though but I can't say when and what they will feature.
We came to the end.I`m wishing you luck,and now I`m giving you a space for the message to the fans!

First of all if you liked the mCD then go get the full-length when it reaches the stores, it will hopefully not let you down. Seconds we would like to show our deepest respect to all our fans in macedonia who support us. THANKS ALOT! ...oh, and thanks for the interview by the way.

item.author: Petar Mihajlovic
item.p: What's bout your name? Why did u named your band like that?
I was pretty into "forest-metal" like Opeth at that moment and I wanted to use the forest-thing with my own music as well hinting at dark melancholy so the name "Forest of Shadows" felt kinda logic.
Why did u start playing?

I had been creating music for quite awhile previous to Forest of Shadows but I had never done anything really official. What made choose an official direction was the fact that I was very depressed at the moment and needed to project my emotional hell on something. To make music and just keep it for yourself doesn't really help you so I decided to make it official.
Short Forest of Shadows history?

FoS was founded in the autumn of 1997 by me (Niclas Frohagen). As a solo-project FoS recorded two albums where one was released in may 1998 as an offical demo called "The Silent Cry". Some weeks after this release I got in contact with Micce Andersson who wanted to join. Since Micce had alot of knowledge about instruments in general as well as production we decided to try and work on our sound. The result was presented on a promo-CD that was released in may 1999. During the following months we started planning to get more members and try to rehearse for some gigs. We recruited Martin Claesson as guitarist in November that year. Previous to this recruitment we had started working on a new recording which would be yet another promo with yet another remake of an old demo-song. It was released in the beginning of 2000. During that time we did our first gig on which we had to use session-musican to complete the setup. Some month later we did yet another gig with a new recruitment on bass. This gig was far from pleasing to us and the main reason was our bass-player who we had to fire. This gig marked the end of our short career as a "live-band" and we decided to focus on the recordings of our coming release, the mCD "where dreams turn to dust". Unfortunatelly things didn't work out with Martin and he had to leave the band before the actuall recordings. The mCD was recorded the summer of 2000 and the idea was to find a label that was willing to release the material. Nothing really happened though and we decided to release the stuff as a demo and to put the songs on mp3.com where we had gain alot of success. For months nothing happened at all in the FoS-camp. In the summer of 2001 we got in contact with Fredrik and Richard Johansson who wanted to join on guitar and drums and we started rehearsing. After some months we realized that it wasn't gonna work out so we decided to drop it. At the same time we got in contact with a new label called Molded Skull Recordings who wanted to release our mCD. The label never managed to release anything though due to financial problems so we had to pick another label. The kings of alternative and extreme metal "Rage of Achilles" had contacted us while we were signed to MS and we contacted them and asked if they were still interessted and so they were. The mCD was released in October 2002. The deal with RoA featured, apart from the mCD, a full-length which we are currently working on. Unfortunatelly things aren't moving very smooth with the recordings and we don't really know when it will be released even though earlier plans said sometime this year (2002).
Influences? First CD u bought was...?

My first CD was the collection "Hero, Hero" by Judas Priest. I don't know if it influenced me to write music though. Maybee it did in some way. Generally speaking I guess I have been influenced by most bands I've heard that play good melodies and create dark and mournfull atmospheres. I guess I speak for Micce there as well. Some influences might be more obvious than others though and the bands that has influenced FoS most over the years are Opeth, Katatonia, My Dying Bride and Anathema.
What r u trying to tell with your music? Inner pain, pure emotions, real life, myths...?

Well if the lyrics were based on real-life then I wouldn't be alive I guess so lets call them pretty dramatic versions of my sour times. FoS doesn't want to preach or moralize or anything we just make music for the sake of art.
Did u gave your best to sound original or that came just like that? Your favorite bands?

We don't care much if we are original or not. As long as we like what we do we're satisfied. There's no point in ripping off other bands though and if there is some part in our music that seems like stolen then it's not done by purpose.
Did u played on some big gigs? Did u open for some other bands or had solo gigs, tour...?
We have just done two gigs and none of them were even close to big. We have never toured or opened for any other band.
Is playing music your only "job"?

Well actually it's not really a job any of us. We do it on our spare time and we do it cuz we feel like it. There are no intentions off making any big money even though it would be a lie to say that we wouldn't like to.
R u pleased with your life in general? Would u change something?
Yeah, I'm pleased with my life as it is in general but still there are things that could be changed. I mean there are always things that you want to change to some extent.
Last one is bout possible playing in Serbia. Would u come n do some gigs here if somebody invites u?
If we start doing gigs again and if someone invites us it would be arrogant to say no if it's not due to very good reasons.

item.author: Nathan Schneider
item.p: Who were your musical inspirations?

On the early stuff, recorded when FoS was still a solo-project, the main inspiration was Opeth, My Dying Bride, Anathema, early Katatonia, Therion and Autumn Tears. The reason why these bands were used as sources of inspiration mainly that they contained the fundamentals of what FoS was to represent, which was (and is) epic and diverse mournfull music. When Micce joined the band, two very important inspirations were added and that was nordic folk-music and simplistic and monotonous metal ala Katatonia (Brave Murder Day). These two inspirations became more and more evident as time passed and were definately the main inspirations on the debute mCD "where dreams turn to dust"
What were your personal inspirations?
Mainly the current situation and how I felt about my life and all that
What is your favorite song that you have written?

If we are talking stuff that is written and recorded the answer would be "Wish". I especially like the lyrics of that one. The new stuff, which is currently being recorded, contains far better songs than "Wish" though IMO.
What instruments do you play?
I play most kinds of guitars and keyboards and I play with my voice. Micce plays more or less all string/keyboard-based instruments. We both play drums fairly.
Do you often get heavy media attention?
No, never.
Do you plan on touring North America?
As it is right now we don't even do any gigs so touring is pretty much out of the question.
What's in the future for you guys?
New and improved records that hopefully will attract more people.
Do you have a website besides the mp3.com/fos one?
www.forestofshadows.com, which is maintained by me.
How long does it take to write a song?

That's rather hard to tell. I mean the basic structures are often written in a day or two (effective time) but the final arrangements are mostly made during the final recordings. The most lengthy part is actually the lyrics 'cause its very hard to just sit down and write something that makes any sense and that fits the gloomy atmosphere of the music. I would guess each lyric takes about a month or so until its considered final. The early lyrics were written in a day or at most a week. I guess we are becoming more pretentious :)
Why are you making music? for Money or for Music?

Since our kind of music doesn't sell all that much it would be rather foolish to make it because of money. We are very happy that we gain some money but we have no intentions to try and make a living of it even though it would be very nice.
How much time do you spend writing?

That depends on how inspired you are and how much spare time that is availible. Personally I think I spend way to little time writing 'cause I have millions of ideas in my head and if I had the time and money I would probably spend all day writing and recording for alot of different projects.

item.author: Alev AlikoÃ§
item.p: First of all I want to thank you for answering the questions. I like to learn about where the name of the group came from or who had the idea and can you describe the music of your band?

I came up with the name "Forest of Shadows" when the band was still a solo-project and it was supposed to symbolize the life I was living back then. Our music can be described as rather slow and melodic dark metal with influences ranging from extreme metal to pop and folk music.

Before "Forest of Shadows" became a two-piece band you had a period that you decided to form solo-projects because of not finding a proper member. You were also not happy with those period's recordings. When you look back can you sum up the situation in those days? Do you think these period helped your own music to became the way it is now?

Definately. During the time as a solo-project I defined the direction in which the band still is heading. The biggest differences between the old and the new stuff is the quality of the arrangements, production and performances. The direction has always been same and shall remain that way.
Which plans do you have about the band in this days? Is there any new album on the way?

Yes, we are currently working on the material for a full-length album and it will probably be released sometime in the autumn of 2002. It is still untitled and contains five songs that all together last for about 60 minutes.
Have you ever give any live performances? And where?

We did two shows in Gothenburg during the spring of 2001 when Martin Claesson was in the band. We weren't very satisfied with these gigs though and when Martin left the band a couple of months later we decided to continue as a studio band.
Which ways do the band use to contact with their fans? How are the reactions about the band and music?

Through email. We get alot of fan-mails from visitors of both our mp3.com page and official website and we try to reply as many as possible. The reactions so far are very positive and alot of people are ecstatic about our music.

Which bands do you listen mostly Niclas? Do these bands effect your music? Because I saw your music archive on your web page and it is a really huge and rich archive. I also know that you're a great fan of Katatonia.

I listen to alot of different bands and Katatonia are one of them. It feels rather meaningless to mention any of the other bands that i listen to since they are so many. I do not consider myself a die-hard Katatonia fan but I like their style and so far every album they have recorded. I guess every band that I listen to (and like) affects the music that I am involved in.
Who prepares the web page of the band? I think it hasn't got enough information for the bands...What's your idea?
I am the webmaster of our site and I think it contains enough information. I mean there's no point in providing too much information cuz that just makes the page messy.
Do other members and you have any other interests? How do you like to spend your time?

Yeah, we have other interests even though music occupies alot of our time. I mean personally I am involved in three active bands and these demand alot of time. As if this wasn't enough I am studying for a master degree in computer science. The remaining hours are in my case spent with my girlfriend or some other friends. Both me and Micce are very into snowboarding and skiing.

I will be very glad to see the band having a live performance in Turkey as a fan of you. Especially whole performing the song " eternal autumn". Do you think about the idea coming in Turkey? Do you know anything about here?
Well, when the day comes when we decide to start playing live again we will ofcourse consider the thought of coming to Turkey.
Thanks again for answering my questions. Do you want to add something to say at last?
Check out our mCD "where dreams turn to dust", which should be out in the stores by now, and stay tuned for our full-length.

item.author: Jack Odel
item.p: Nicke could you first tell the viewers of this interview a bit about Forest of Shadows and the early time of you starting out?

Forest of Shadows was founded by me as a solo-project back in 1997. In may 1998 the first demo "The Silent Cry" was released and some weeks after that, multi-instrumentalist Micce Andersson joined the band. We released a teaser of our "new sound" in 1999 titled "Promotion-CD 1999" and it gained quite good critics so we decided to start rehearsing and seeking some new members for a live-setting. In the Autumn of 1999 Martin Claesson joined on guitar and the three of us plus some session-members did two gigs in 2000 before Martin left the band and me and Micce decided to go for the studio-band thing for awhile since we weren't all to satisfied with our live-performances. During the time as a "live-band" we released the teaser "Promotion-CD 2000" and wrote the material for the upcoming demo "Where Dreams turn to Dust" which we released in the autumn of 2000. Some month after this release we inked a deal with the new label "Molded Skull" who were to release our demo as a mCD. But things didn't go as planned for the label and in April 2001 they wrote and recommended us to find another label. And so we did and ended up with Rage of Achilles (the new promise among labels) and they released our mCD "Where Dreams turn to Dust" on the 29th of October 2001 and that's pretty much where we are right now...
Why metal? Why not pop or grunge? Is there anything specifically particular that attracted you to metal?

The main reason why FoS became a metal-band is that metal was more or less the only genre that mattered to me when I founded the band back in 1997. The most imortant thing about FoS being a metal band is the creative freedom which ofcourse has limitations but still makes it possible to develop a quite original and personal sound without to much effort. Since Micce joined in 1998 we have oped our sound more and more away from standardized doom-metal towards some kind of utopian FoS-sound. Where we are right now metal is more of a founding layer and a powerful way of expressing something that might as well be pop, ambient or whatever.
Forest of Shadows covers a vast musical spectrum and is impossible to define within an existing genre of metal? You happy with that?

Personally I'm very happy with that. There are both good and bad sides about having a label sticked on your music. The good thing is that you get alot of easy promotion which can be quite handy. I mean if you get to know about a band that sound like one of your favorites then you'll probably check that band out. The bad thing is that people don't listen to what the music really has to offer provided that it has something to offer.

With the countless musician changes that Forest of Shadows have gone through it must have been an awful weight upon your shoulders to hold all Forest of Shadows responsibilities for a quite a proportion of time. Any thoughts?

Well most of these persons were just in the band for some weeks. The only person that's not in the band any more that really mattered is Martin Claesson. Being the founding-member I have had and still do have alot of responsibillity that FoS stays on the "right path" and continues to represent that specific feeling that makes it FoS. Since I'm not all that keen on dictatorshit I try to share the responsibillity as much as possible though.
With the release of the MCD "Where Dreams turn to Dust", the press reaction was one of joy and excitement, were you happy with amount of support the MCD received?

I don't know if the mCD as such has gained that much support. The most important channel of promotion has been the songs on mp3.com without which we would never have reached as far as we have done. Maybee what the press wrote made people visit mp3.com but if that's the case then it's not just thanks to the mCD.
What can you tell us about the soon to be released album? You said it possesses the same style as music as "Where Dreams turn to Dust", but a bit more 'well-done', care to elaborate at all?

Well most of the material that is to be used for the upcoming full-length was written during the WDTTD-period so in that sense it's quite similar to the mCD. One of the songs is actually taken from the very first demo as well. What I meant by more "well-done" is that this time we know that the stuff is to be released which we didn't when we recorded WDTTD and thus it feels more motivated to put as much effort as possible in the process. It will also be more "well-done" since we have had plenty of time to really analyze the material and get into it in order to get the best out of it. Another important thing is that while the mCD was more or less written by me alone this album shows more of a cooperation between me and micce.
On the new album. Are there any album titles that you have brewing in your mind at the moment or is it still an opened question?
We haven't even started to think about it so I would say it's a still an opened question indeed.
Is the recording of the new album going well?
So far we just been playing around with the material so we haven't really recorded anything that is to be used. But I would say that the entire process is going quite according to plans.

With influences from Katatonia and an excellent cover of 'Rainroom' do you draw you inspiration from the earlier Katatonia albums ("Dance of December Souls" and "Brave Murder Day") or more of the newer Katatonia albums ("Discourage Ones" and "Last Fair Deal Gone Down")?

Even though both me and Micce adore Katatonia and especially their legendary album "Brave Murder Day" we are far from trying to sound like them. The main influence we have got from them is their way of making simplistic and still VERY powerfull music which is most evident on BMD. I think the BMD-style is more suitable for FoS than the newer stuff.

You must be so happy that Rage of Achilles signed you up. Are things working out ok as far as distribution and promotional activities? As I read in your biographical information that they have a real interest in Forest of Shadows and that must really make it easier for you in terms of creative directions.

When we were contacted by Rage of Achilles for the first time we didn't know anything about them apart from the fact that another band in which I play, Shubend, was signed to them as well. But soon they started to show up in alot of zines and alot of their bands recieved good reviews especially Cult of Luna, which is a band both me and Micce like alot. Still the best thing about Rage of Achilles is that they care alot about us and give us creative freedom. The only ones that seems to fuck up the process is the distros, who are slow as hell, and I guess I can't blame Rage of Achilles for that. To conclude we are VERY happy with the fact that Rage of Achilles signed us.
What CDs are spinning in your CD player at the moment?
"A Fine Day To Exit" - Anathema, "Promotheus" - Emperor, "Director's Cut" - Fantomas and "Dreadful Hours" - My Dying Bride

The metal scene is one that is constantly fluctuating between trends. Mid to middle-late 90's there was the big jump of symphonic black metal bands. Late 90's and up until now there seems to be a large surge of melodic death metal. What are your thoughts on things and how they stand at the moment? Any predictions to how the metal scene in Europe will change?

There will always be trends in music and I don't see anything bad about it. It makes the trendy genre grow and most of the time it grows into something good. I believe that symphonic black metal is as big today as it has ever been. I would rather call it symphonic metal or something though cuz to me symphonic black metal is pretty far from true (tm) black metal like mayhem, darkthrone and burzum. Well actually I don't care much for thoose labels anyway. Over to my predictions... hmm... well I think (and hope) alot of bands will become more pretentious which means that there will be more "art-metal".

Being a My Dying Bride fan, and having My Dying Bride influence Forest of Shadows, are you happy My Dying Bride returned to the sound of "Turn Loose the Swans"? And what do you think of "The Dreadful Hours"?

Well in my opinion "The Angel and the Dark River" is almost as good as "Turn Loose the Swans" which means that I didn't really mind weather they returned to their roots or not. In my opinion they never really left their path apart from on "34.788%" which in my opinion doesn't really represent what MDB should be about even though it's a good album. About "The Dreadful Hours" then... well I like it alot especially since it shows a couple of new directions that are quite interessting.
Which band would you most like to tour with, and why?

If we start playing live again we will try and do something different and not just play... it will probably be more of an art thing or something. Anyway, if that is the case then the other bands we tour with need to fit in such an arrangement and the only band that I can up with right now that fit are Shape of Despair.
Has press promotion (like www.metalbite.com) helped much with Forest of Shadows or more www.mp3.com?

There is a big difference between how mp3.com and the press promote us. The amount of promotion we get from the press depends on what the press writes while mp3.com is more connected to the music since they do promotion by playing our music. mp3.com has helped us more than we ever could dream of. I mean there were days when we had about 1000 downloads/day and judging from the huge amount of fan-mails we have recieved at least some of thoose who downloaded the songs loved what they heard. Still a very good review in a huge zine could easily beat 1000 downloads/day since most people are enslaved by what others think. And finally I wouldn't mind getting more reviews like the one you did :)
Where do you see Forest of Shadows five years down the track?
Forest of Shadows will definately exist in five years but I have no clue what we will sound like or who will be involved apart from me.
Finally, I thank you very much Nicke for this interview and Forest of Shadows for their excellent metal music. Would you like to say anything to anyone specifically or generally?

And I thank you Jack for a great interview (and a great review as well) and I would like to tell everyone to check out our mCD "Where dreams turn to Dust" which is out now and finally I would, on behalf of Forest of Shadows, like to thank everyone that has supported us and downloaded our songs from mp3.com. THANKS ALOT!!!

item.author: Black Widow
item.p: Who are the members of your band and what is their instrument?
Niclas Frohagen - Vocals, guitar, synth and programming; Micce Andersson - Bass, guitar, synth, violin and programming.
Where do you hail from?
Gothenburg, Sweden
How long have you played together?
The band has been a two-piece since the summer of 1998 so figure it out.
How did you meet?
Thru a common friend.
How do you categorize yourselves, or do you?
We prefer not to but the audience demands some kind of label so we usually categorize us as dark-metal.
Who have been your influences in music?
In alphabetical order: Anathema, Jan Johansson, Katatonia, My Dying Bride, Opeth and Shape of Despair.
Have you released a CD?
We are to release our first "real" album, the EP "where dreams turn to dust", sometime before the end of this year (2000). Previous to that we have released some demos.
Do you tour? If so, where can we find you next?
No
Which member of your band is the press hound?
Me, Niclas Frohagen
Tell me your favourite band story.

I guess that would be from our first gig when our session bass-player didn't show up due to an accident and we had to find a new one and rehearse the set with him in a couple of hours. Due to the circumstances it went pretty well but still it was very weird.
Is there anything in the works that you can share with us?
We are writing some new songs and enhancing some old demo-songs for a coming full-length album.
How was the band named?
The band was named back in 1997 when it was my (Niclas Frohagen) solo-project. The Forest of Shadows was supposed to symbolize the life I was (and am) living.
What year did this band first come together?
1997 as a solo-project of mine (Niclas Frohagen)
Thank you for visiting Night Shade, we'll look forward to seeing you again in the near future. Night Shade wishes you the best of luck and the darkest of shadows.
